name: Secret de sa Figuera
description: Located near Cala Magraner, this cave is notable for both its natural and archaeological value, harboring evidence of prehistoric human use including modifications to its access and interior. Features include a hall possibly related to historical smuggling activities and a prehistorically used entrance hall. The fig tree that gave the cave its name has since disappeared.
full_description:
The Cova des Secret de sa Figuera, located in the Cala Magraner area, is known for its natural and archaeological value, harboring evidence of anthropic use that includes modifications to its access or interior, characteristic of the prehistoric caves of the island.

Fig tree that gives the cavity its name, currently disappeared.

Room conditioned, possibly related to smuggling.

Entrance hall, of prehistoric use.
